methods.description: Standard housing and care conditions for adolescent male Wistar rats used in the study, adhering to ethical guidelines.

steps.description: Adolescent male Wistar rats, aged 50-60 days, were obtained from the animal facility of King Fahd Research Center, King Abdulaziz University, Jeddah, Saudi Arabia.
outputs: Adolescent male Wistar rats (50-60 days old)

steps.description: They were used in the study according to the guidelines of the Biochemical and Research Ethics Committee at King Abdulaziz University, in accordance with the NIH guidelines.
steps.searchable_summary: Ensure animal use complies with ethical guidelines.

steps.description: Animals were housed in a well-ventilated, temperature-controlled room at 22 ± 3°C with a 12 h light-dark cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: House animals under controlled environmental conditions.

steps.description: They were provided with standard rat chow pellets and tap water.
steps.searchable_summary: Provide standard food and water ad libitum.

steps.description: All experimental procedures were performed between 8-10 a.m.
steps.searchable_summary: Schedule experimental procedures within a specific time window.

steps.description: care was taken to avoid stressful conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Implement measures to minimize animal stress.

methods.description: Aseptic surgical procedure to remove testes and epididymides in adolescent male Wistar rats to induce a castrated state.

steps.description: Orchidectomy was performed aseptically, under ethyl ether anesthesia...
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare for aseptic surgery and administer anesthesia.

steps.description: Following ligation of the spermatic cord and vessels...
steps.searchable_summary: Ligate the spermatic cord and vessels.

steps.description: The remaining stump was pushed back through the inguinal canal into the abdominal cavity...
steps.searchable_summary: Reposition the remaining stump into the abdominal cavity.

steps.description: ...and the scrotal sac was closed by sutures [15].
steps.searchable_summary: Close the scrotal sac with sutures.

steps.description: After castration, the rats were maintained under standard laboratory conditions for 7 days in order to allow a definite involution of the prostatic gland [16].
steps.searchable_summary: Maintain castrated rats for 7 days post-surgery for prostatic involution.

methods.description: Procedure to induce Androgen-induced Prostatic Hyperplasia (APH) in castrated rats by administering testosterone propionate and citral over 30 days.

steps.description: APH was induced as previously described by Engelstein et al. [17] in castrated rats using citral and testosterone.
steps.searchable_summary: Use castrated rats for APH induction.
outputs: Castrated rats ready for APH induction

steps.description: Rats were subcutaneously injected with testosterone propionate in corn oil (0.5 mg/0.1 ml/rat) each day for 30 days.
steps.searchable_summary: Administer testosterone propionate subcutaneously daily for 30 days.

steps.description: Citral, 1 M diluted in 70% ethanol, was smeared on a different shaved area of skin, each time, on the back at a final dose of 185 mg/kg every 4 th day for 30 days.
steps.searchable_summary: Administer citral topically (smeared on shaved skin) every 4th day for 30 days.

steps.description: Control groups were smeared with the solvent (ethanol) alone at the shaved skin.
steps.searchable_summary: Administer solvent control (ethanol) topically to control group rats.
outputs: Control group rats receiving topical solvent

steps.description: Considering the pungent lime fragrance of citral, the control groups were kept in a separate location from the citral-treated animals to avoid possible false results as citral have some influence via the olfactory tract [17].
steps.searchable_summary: Separate control and treatment groups spatially to avoid olfactory cross-contamination.
outputs: Spatially separated treatment and control groups
